decisions.summary: Add a single status-aware resolver `resolveConversationTranscript(cwd, claudeSessionId) -> { path, status }` (status: 'ok' | 'expired' | 'unknown') in a new module `src/lib/conversations/transcript-path.ts`. It is built on the existing `sessionFilePath()` primitive in src/lib/paths.ts (the encode+join is the shared single source of truth for derivation) and adds the on-disk existence check + the `*/<sessionId>.jsonl` glob fallback + the 3-state status. The CLI command and the conv-lookup skill both resolve through this one path.

Command is `pan conv jsonl <conv-id>` with `transcript` as an alias. It resolves a CONVERSATION id (the /conv/<N> number) via getConversationById() — deliberately distinct from `pan conv show <id>`, which operates on discovered-session ids (getDiscoveredSessionById).

Exit/output contract: plain mode prints the absolute path to stdout and exits 0 on status 'ok'; on 'expired' prints a retention-explaining message to stderr and exits 1; on 'unknown' prints a 'no claude_session_id recorded' message to stderr and exits 1. `--json` mode prints a single JSON object { status, path, conversationId, claudeSessionId, cwd } to stdout and exits 0 regardless of status (the caller reads .status). This mirrors conv-find.py --jsonl's plain-mode behavior and matches how the existing `pan conv show --json` / `current --json` return 0 with data in the payload.

Preserve the glob fallback from conv-find.py: when the derived path (`sessionFilePath(cwd, sessionId)`) is absent, search `~/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` (one directory level only) and return the first match as 'ok'. This keeps cwd-encoding-drift / moved-workspace cases resolvable, which is part of why conv 2598-class lookups failed before.

Update the conv-lookup skill in the SAME change (per docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md). The tracked source of truth is `sync-sources/skills/conv-lookup/` (the `.claude/skills/` copy is gitignored and regenerated by `pan sync`). conv-find.py's resolve_session_file() delegates to `pan conv jsonl --json <id>` and the duplicated encode/derive/glob logic is removed; SKILL.md documents `pan conv jsonl` / `pan conv transcript` as the canonical resolver.

Do NOT refactor the dashboard (ws-rpc.ts:609) or the claude-code transcript adapter (transcript-adapter.ts:103) to route through the new resolver. They already build on the shared `sessionFilePath()` primitive, return string|null (no status needed), and live on the fork/handoff hot path; adding the glob fallback there would be a behavior change out of scope for this issue. The 'single source of truth' the issue asks for is satisfied: derivation = sessionFilePath(), status-resolution = the new helper, both single-sourced.

hazards.summary: Numeric-id collision: `pan conv jsonl <N>` takes a CONVERSATION id (the /conv/<N> number) while `pan conv show <N>` takes a discovered-session id. An agent could pass the wrong kind of id and get a confusing miss.
hazards.mitigation: Command description and SKILL.md must state explicitly that the argument is the conversation id (the number in pan.localhost/conv/<N>). Keep the not-found error message clear (e.g. 'Conversation <N> not found').

hazards.summary: Delegating conv-find.py to `pan conv jsonl` introduces a new hard dependency on the `pan` binary being on PATH. Previously conv-find.py was pure Python + sqlite with no external process dependency.
hazards.mitigation: resolve_session_file() must handle FileNotFoundError (pan not on PATH) and non-zero/malformed output with a clear single-line error to stderr — NOT a Python stack trace, and NOT a silent re-introduction of the removed inline derivation (that would defeat the single-source-of-truth goal). In real Panopticon agent sessions `pan` is always installed.

hazards.summary: Pi-harness conversations have claude_session_id = NULL; their transcripts live under ~/.panopticon/agents/<tmux>/sessions/, not ~/.claude/projects/. The new resolver will report them as 'unknown'.
hazards.mitigation: Intentionally out of scope — the issue frames resolution as claude_session_id + cwd. Report 'unknown' honestly. A follow-up can extend the resolver to Pi via getTranscriptAdapter(harness).resolveSessionFile(). Note this in the command help only if cheap.

hazards.summary: The `--json` exit code (always 0) differs from plain mode (0 on ok, 1 otherwise). A script that checks the exit code of `pan conv jsonl --json` expecting failure on expired/unknown would be wrong.
hazards.mitigation: Document the contract in the command help and SKILL.md: in --json mode read the `status` field, not the exit code. The conv-find.py delegation parses the payload's status, not $?.

sessionHistory.note: Initial planning session (auto mode) for PAN-1712: pan conv jsonl <conv-id> first-class transcript path resolution
